featured.title: Kythik Since ’99
featured.blurb: A living MMO memory vault: old-school raiding, class identity, server culture, and the stories that made early online worlds feel dangerous, social, and strange.

featured.title: Raid Stories, Not Résumé Soup
featured.blurb: The point is the feel of the era: reputation, pressure, jank, late nights, clutch pulls, and the people who made games feel bigger than the map.

quickPaths.title: Era Timeline
quickPaths.blurb: A player-first route through early MMO history: what mattered, why it mattered, and how the culture changed.

quickPaths.title: Raid Memory Vault
quickPaths.blurb: Long-form stories about pressure, roles, guild expectations, and the weird social contract of hardcore raiding.

quickPaths.title: Hello Gamer, Old School Edition
quickPaths.blurb: A landing spot for viewers who want the MMO-history side of the stream without digging through modern ARPG pages.

archive.title: Why EverQuest Felt Different
archive.blurb: Danger, downtime, travel friction, and server reputation created stories before games learned to label everything as content.

archive.title: Necromancer Brain, Forever
archive.blurb: A love letter to solo routes, utility, weird power curves, and the specific kind of player who enjoys winning by understanding systems better.

archive.title: The Pressure of Being Useful
archive.blurb: Hardcore raiding was not just boss mechanics. It was preparation, trust, logs before logs, and being someone the raid could count on.

archive.title: Server Legends and Forum Ghosts
archive.blurb: The old internet made player reputations feel permanent. Sometimes that was magic. Sometimes it was cursed. Often it was both.

resources.title: Kythik on Twitch
resources.blurb: Live ARPG grinding, MMO stories, and high-yap strategy talk.

resources.title: Kythik on YouTube
resources.blurb: Stream cuts, guides, and long-form content as the archive grows.
